title: astragalus crotalariae
intro: Astragalus crotalariae, also known as the Crotalaria or Rattlepod, is a plant species in the legume family, characterized by its distinctive rattle-producing seed pods. The medicinal properties of Astragalus crotalariae are attributed to its alkaloid content, particularly crotamine, which has been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and exhibiting potential antitumor activity. In traditional Chinese medicine, Astragalus crotalariae is often prepared into teas, infusions, or decoctions, and may also be used in combination with other herbs for its purported medicinal effects.
